Today is April 6, 2026. Savannah Guthrie has returned to the NBC Today show while the search for her missing mother continues. In an interview she gave last month, what specific reason did she give for her decision to return to work?
(A) She stated that she had to return because the show and her colleagues are her family.
(B) She stated that she was now certain she would be able to perform her duties despite the circumstances.
(C) She stated that she returned to use her platform to find the suspect who took her mother.
(D) She stated that she returned because her mother had been found safe in Tucson, Arizona.
(E) She stated that she returned to lead NBC's coverage of the upcoming Winter Olympics.
explanation: In an interview last month with Today, Savannah Guthrie stated, "I can't not come back. This is my family," referring to her colleagues and the show.
model_output:
In an interview with her former co-host Hoda Kotb that aired in March 2026, Savannah Guthrie discussed the daunting prospect of returning to the *Today* show while her mother, Nancy Guthrie, remained missing. She explained her decision to return by stating, "It's hard to imagine doing it because it's such a place of joy and lightness, and I can't come back and try to be something that I'm not. But I can't not come back, because it's my family". 

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is April 6, 2026. A juvenile gray whale nicknamed "Willapa Willy" was recently found dead in a Washington state river. According to the Cascadia Research Collective, what was the whale's condition when it was first spotted in the north fork of the river?
(A) It was suffering from significant trauma to the head consistent with a ship strike.
(B) It was thin but behaving normally and showed no signs of injuries.
(C) It was entangled in heavy fishing gear and unable to navigate the river.
(D) It was an adult male measuring 42 feet in length and was severely malnourished.
(E) It was already deceased and floating near the north fork of the river.
explanation: The article states that when the juvenile whale was first spotted last Wednesday, it "although thin, was behaving normally," with "no sign of injuries."
model_output:
According to the Cascadia Research Collective, when the juvenile gray whale nicknamed "Willapa Willy" was first spotted in the North Fork of the Willapa River, it was observed to be thin but behaving normally and did not appear to have any injuries. The research group initially monitored the whale, hoping it would find its way back out of the river on its own, before it was unfortunately found deceased a few days later. 

*(Note: The details regarding a 42-foot adult male with significant head trauma from a ship strike refer to a different gray whale found dead around the same time near Ocean Shores.)*

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Daily Chronicle (https://www.chronline.com/stories/gray-whale-seen-20-miles-inland-dies-in-willapa-river,338081)

- The Spokesman-Review (https://www.spokesman.com/stories/2026/apr/06/gray-whale-seen-20-miles-inland-dies-in-southwest-/)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The Artemis II astronauts are scheduled to pass behind the Moon today, resulting in a communication blackout. How does the expected duration of this blackout compare to the isolation experienced by Michael Collins during the Apollo 11 mission?
(A) The Artemis blackout will last 48 minutes, which is the same duration as the Apollo 11 blackout.
(B) The Artemis blackout will last 48 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 40 minutes.
(C) The Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.
(D) The Artemis blackout will last 24 hours, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.
(E) The Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 mission maintained a constant connection.
explanation: The article states that the Artemis astronauts will be out of contact for about 40 minutes, while Michael Collins' contact vanished for 48 minutes during Apollo 11.
model_output:
Based on the mission profiles for both spaceflights, the Artemis II spacecraft (Orion) is expected to experience a communication blackout of approximately 40 minutes as it passes behind the far side of the Moon, blocking radio and laser signals to Earth. 

In comparison, during the Apollo 11 mission in 1969, Command Module Pilot Michael Collins experienced a communication blackout that lasted 48 minutes during each of his solo lunar orbits while Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin were on the Moon's surface. 

Therefore, the Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- India Times: "Why Artemis II astronauts lost contact with Earth for 40 minutes during its Moon mission" (https://www.indiatimes.com)
- NASA: "Artemis II Flight Day 6: Crew Ready for Lunar Flyby" (https://www.nasa.gov)
- India Times: "Artemis II astronauts lose contact with Earth for 40 minutes in space" (https://www.indiatimes.com)
- National Today: "Artemis Astronauts Face 40-Minute Blackout on Far Side of Moon" (https://nationaltoday.com)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Donald Trump has endorsed Steve Hilton for the governorship of California. According to the report, what is Hilton's history regarding his status in the United States?
(A) He has been a naturalized US citizen since he first moved to California in 2010.
(B) He is a British citizen who is currently serving as a top aide to David Cameron.
(C) He was granted honorary US citizenship by President Trump in a Truth Social post.
(D) He stated in 2019 that he was in the process of applying for US citizenship.
(E) He is a dual citizen of the UK and US who has lived in California since 1832.
explanation: The article mentions that Hilton joined Stanford University as a visiting scholar in 2012 and "said he was applying for US citizenship in 2019."
model_output:
Based on the available information regarding Steve Hilton's background and citizenship status:

* **(A) is incorrect** because he moved to California in 2012 (not 2010) and did not become a naturalized citizen until 2021. 
* **(B) is incorrect** because he is a *former* aide to David Cameron, having left his position as director of strategy in 2012.
* **(C) is incorrect** because he is a naturalized citizen, not an honorary one granted status via a Truth Social post.
* **(E) is incorrect** because he has not lived in California since 1832 (which is historically impossible for him). 

**(D) is the correct statement.** During a July 2019 broadcast of his Fox News show *The Next Revolution*, Hilton announced to his viewers that he had officially begun the process of applying for U.S. citizenship. He later completed the process and was naturalized in May 2021 [1, 2]. 

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [1] Fox News (July 08, 2019) (https://www.foxnews.com/opinion/steve-hilton-proud-americans-thats-not-who-todays-democrats-are)
- [2] TIME (April 06, 2026) (https://time.com/7028181/steve-hilton-california-governor-trump-endorsement/)

Today is April 6, 2026. The US Supreme Court has issued an order that could lead to the dismissal of Steve Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction. What specific reason was cited in the brief unsigned order for sending the case back to the lower court?
(A) That Bannon had already completed his four-month prison sentence in Connecticut.
(B) That the original conviction violated Bannon's constitutional right to a third term.
(C) That the US Solicitor General D. John Sauer had personally pardoned Bannon.
(D) That the January 2021 Capitol riot subpoenas were found to be legally invalid.
(E) The existence of a pending motion to dismiss the indictment.
explanation: The article states: "The brief unsigned order from the Supreme Court cited 'the pending motion to dismiss the indictment'."
model_output:
On April 6, 2026, the US Supreme Court issued a brief, unsigned order that vacated a lower court's decision upholding Steve Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction. The Supreme Court sent the case back to the US Court of Appeals for the DC Circuit for further consideration specifically "in light of the pending motion to dismiss the indictment". This action cleared the way for the Justice Department to move forward with dismissing the criminal case against him. 

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- SCOTUSblog (https://www.scotusblog.com/2026/04/court-allows-steve-bannon-to-move-forward-on-dismissal-of-criminal-charges-against-him/)
- WHTC (https://whtc.com/2026/04/06/us-supreme-court-clears-way-for-dismissal-of-case-against-trump-ally-steve-bannon/)
- The Straits Times (https://www.straitstimes.com/world/united-states/us-supreme-court-clears-way-for-dismissal-of-case-against-trump-ally-steve-bannon)

Today is April 6, 2026. The US Secret Service is investigating reports of gunfire near the White House that occurred early Sunday morning. Where specifically did officers conduct a search after responding to the reports?
(A) The area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park.
(B) The grounds of the Mar-a-Lago estate in Florida.
(C) The interior of the Oval Office where the President was working.
(D) The Qasem Soleimani international airport in south-western Iran.
(E) A residential building in the Israeli city of Haifa.
explanation: The article states that officers "responded to reports of gunfire in the area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park just after midnight local time... and conducted a search of the park."
model_output:
Based on reports from April 5, 2026, the US Secret Service investigated reports of overnight gunfire near the White House. Officers responded shortly after midnight to the vicinity of Lafayette Park, which is located directly north of the White House. Following the reports, authorities conducted a thorough search of Lafayette Park and the surrounding area, though no suspects or victims were found. 

Therefore, the officers conducted their search in the area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. President Trump has issued a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. According to his most recent social media updates, when exactly is the current deadline set to expire?
(A) Monday, April 6, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.
(B) Tuesday, April 7, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.
(C) Wednesday, April 8, at midnight local time in Tehran.
(D) Within 48 hours of his initial March 21 announcement.
(E) Friday, April 10, to coincide with the rescue of the F-15 pilot.
explanation: The article notes that on Sunday (April 5), Trump posted "Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time!", which was an extension of the deadline originally meant to expire on Monday, April 6.
model_output:
Based on recent reports from April 2026, President Trump issued an ultimatum to Iran regarding the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. In a series of social media posts on Truth Social on Sunday, April 5, he threatened to bomb Iranian power plants and bridges if the strait was not reopened. In a follow-up post, he specifically clarified that the deadline for Iran to comply is set for Tuesday, April 7, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time. 

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- TIME (https://time.com)
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com)
- Axios (https://www.axios.com)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Details have emerged regarding the rescue of a US F-15 crew member in Iran. According to media reports, what specific role did the CIA play in the success of this operation?
(A) They provided the A-10 Warthog aircraft that protected the rescue helicopters.
(B) They negotiated a temporary ceasefire with Gen Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi.
(C) They tracked the airman's location and conducted a deception campaign.
(D) They personally extracted the airman from the 7,000ft mountain ridge.
(E) They destroyed two transport planes to prevent them from falling into enemy hands.
explanation: The article states: "Media reports said the CIA played a crucial role in the rescue by tracking the airman and passing his exact location to the Pentagon. The agency engaged in a deception campaign inside Iran..."
model_output:
Based on media reports from April 2026 regarding the rescue of the downed US F-15 crew member in Iran, the CIA played a critical intelligence and psychological warfare role. Specifically, the CIA tracked the stranded airman's location in a mountain crevice and passed the exact coordinates to the Pentagon. Simultaneously, they launched an extensive deception campaign, spreading false information within Iran that the airman had already been found and was being moved overland in a convoy for exfiltration. This misinformation confused Iranian forces and diverted their search efforts, allowing US special operations forces to successfully extract the airman from the 7,000-foot mountain ridge. 

The actual extraction was carried out by US military special forces (such as Navy SEALs), and the destruction of the malfunctioning transport planes was also conducted by the US military, not the CIA.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- jpost.com (https://www.jpost.com)
- time.com (https://time.com)
- cbsnews.com (https://www.cbsnews.com)
- the-star.co.ke (https://www.the-star.co.ke)
- telegraphindia.com (https://www.telegraphindia.com)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The US has arrested Hamideh Soleimani Afshar, a relative of the late Iranian General Qasem Soleimani. According to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), what evidence illustrated that her asylum claims were fraudulent?
(A) She entered the United States on a student visa in 2015.
(B) She was found to be the primary architect of the "roadside bomb."
(C) She failed to apply for a green card after being granted asylum in 2019.
(D) She visited Iran four times after receiving her US green card.
(E) She was an outspoken critic of the totalitarian regime in Iran.
explanation: The DHS stated that although she was granted asylum in 2019, she revealed in a 2025 naturalization application that "she had visited Iran four times since receiving her green card."
model_output:
According to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), the evidence illustrating that Hamideh Soleimani Afshar's asylum claims were fraudulent was that she traveled back to Iran multiple times after obtaining her legal status. 

Afshar originally entered the U.S. on a tourist visa in 2015 and was granted asylum in 2019 under the premise that she could not safely return to her home country. However, during a 2025 naturalization application, she disclosed that she had made at least four separate return trips to Iran after receiving her green card. DHS officials stated that these voluntary return trips directly contradicted her claims of persecution, rendering her initial asylum claims fraudulent. 

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Fox 11 Los Angeles (https://www.foxla.com)
- Los Angeles Times (https://www.latimes.com)
- Azat TV (https://azat.tv)
- Parties365 (https://parties365.com)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Residents of Social Circle, Georgia, are protesting a proposed ICE detention center. What specific reason did City Manager Eric Taylor give for his decision to shut off the water supply to the warehouse?
(A) The warehouse was not built for human habitation and lacked proper ventilation.
(B) The town's sewage system, established in 1962, had already completely failed.
(C) The federal government refused to pay the $130 million purchase price for the site.
(D) The facility was located within a five-minute drive of a local elementary school.
(E) He wanted to prevent full access to the city's water supply until the impact was understood.
explanation: Eric Taylor told the BBC: "If you open up that water meter, it gives them full access to the entire supply of the whole city. I can't let that happen without knowing what the ultimate impact is going to be."
model_output:
To determine the correct answer, we can look at the statements made by Social Circle City Manager Eric Taylor regarding the proposed ICE detention center. 

When explaining his decision to place a physical lock on the warehouse's water meter, Taylor stated that the lock would remain in place "until ICE indicates how water and sewer will be served without exceeding our limited infrastructure capacity". The city's wastewater system was already operating at its maximum capacity, and the massive facility—which was projected to house up to 10,000 detainees—would effectively triple the town's population and overburden its resources. Therefore, he shut off the water to force the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and ICE to address the infrastructural impact before moving forward. 

While the town's sewage system is older (dating back to 1962) and maxed out, it had not "completely failed" (eliminating B). The federal government did, in fact, purchase the site for nearly $130 million (eliminating C). While the site is near an elementary school, this was not the specific reason given for the utility shut-off (eliminating D). 

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Washington Examiner (https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/news/georgia-officials-thwart-social-circle-ice-facility-by-turning-off-water)
- The Covington News (https://www.covnews.com/news/local-news/council-backs-social-circle-city-managers-decision-to-lock-ice-facility-water-meter/)
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/18/georgia-ice-detention-center-social-circle)
- 11Alive (https://www.11alive.com/article/news/local/social-circle-ice-facility-water-meter-locked/85-123456789)
- FOX 5 Atlanta (https://www.fox5atlanta.com/news/city-locks-social-circle-ice-facilitys-utility-box)
- Georgia Public Broadcasting (https://www.gpb.org/news/2026/03/17/social-circle-puts-ice-warehouse-plan-on-ice-water-meter-lock)

Today is April 6, 2026. Despite President Trump's declaration of "overwhelming air dominance" in Iran, military planners remain concerned about specific Iranian capabilities. Which weapon system is mentioned as a particular threat to low-flying US aircraft?
(A) Man portable air defence systems (Manpads).
(B) Heavy surface-to-air missiles (SAMs) stationed at Kharg Island.
(C) Ballistic missiles capable of reaching residential buildings in Haifa.
(D) Iranian drones and cruise missiles used for defensive roles.
(E) Small arms fire from petrochemical facilities in Abu Dhabi.
explanation: The article mentions that operations "risk exposing US forces to lingering Iranian capabilities, including difficult-to-find man portable air defence systems, known as Manpads... most effective against low-flying aircraft."
model_output:
Based on the reports from April 2026 regarding the ongoing US-Iran conflict scenario, President Trump declared "overwhelming air dominance and superiority" following the successful search-and-rescue operations for downed US F-15 crew members. 

However, military planners and analysts cautioned that the airspace remains highly contested and dangerous. Specifically, they warned that any complex ground operations or low-altitude missions would expose US forces to lingering Iranian defensive capabilities. The primary threat cited against low-flying US aircraft and helicopters in these reports is **man-portable air defense systems (Manpads)**, which are difficult-to-find, shoulder-launched missiles. 

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- lifetoolkit.tech (https://lifetoolkit.tech/trump-declares-victory-after-rescue-but-threats-to-us-operation-in-iran-still-loom/)
- USA Herald (https://usaherald.com/trump-hails-pilot-rescue-as-victory-while-risks-in-iran-persist/)
- Heal From Zero (https://healfromzero.com/analysis-trump-declares-victory-in-iran-war-after-rescue-but-threats-to-us-operation-still-loom/)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Canada is moving forward with a large-scale gun buy-back program for "assault-style" weapons. According to the current government timeline, what is the deadline for these firearms to be destroyed?
(A) March 31, 2026.
(B) October 30, 2026.
(C) June 2, 2026.
(D) December 31, 2026.
(E) February 14, 2027.
explanation: The article states: "while an amnesty period for gun owners has been extended several times, the deadline is now 30 October for the firearms to be destroyed."
model_output:
Based on the current timeline for Canada's Assault-Style Firearms Compensation Program, the deadline for these firearms to be disposed of, surrendered, or permanently deactivated (destroyed) is **October 30, 2026**. 

While the deadline for gun owners to *declare* their intent to participate in the financial compensation program was March 31, 2026, the federal amnesty period protecting owners of the banned firearms from criminal liability officially expires on October 30, 2026. By this date, all prohibited "assault-style" weapons must be permanently deactivated or handed over, otherwise the owners will be in illegal possession of a prohibited firearm and could face criminal charges.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. Gen Christopher LaNeve has been named the acting chief of staff of the US Army. Which role did LaNeve hold immediately prior to his appointment as vice-chief of staff in February 2026?
(A) Commander of the 82nd Airborne Division.
(B) Commander of the Eighth Army in South Korea.
(C) Senior military assistant to Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.
(D) Vice-chief of staff under Gen Randy George.
(E) Acting chief of staff replacing James Mingus.
explanation: The article states: "Prior to that [his appointment as vice-chief], LaNeve was a senior military assistant to Hegseth, a role he had been in since April 2025."
model_output:
To determine Gen. Christopher LaNeve's role immediately prior to his appointment as vice-chief of staff, we can look at his recent career timeline:

*   **March 2022 – November 2023:** Commanded the 82nd Airborne Division.
*   **April 2024 – April 2025:** Served as the Commanding General of the Eighth Army in South Korea.
*   **April 2025 – February 2026:** Served as the Senior Military Assistant to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. 
*   **February 2026:** Appointed as the Vice Chief of Staff of the US Army, replacing Gen. James Mingus.
*   **April 2026:** Named Acting Chief of Staff of the US Army following the ousting of Gen. Randy George.

Immediately prior to becoming the vice-chief of staff in February 2026, LaNeve was serving as the senior military assistant to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. 

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

(C) It was a sign of solidarity with the Artemis II moon mission crew.

explanation: The article states: "She wore a bright yellow dress, which NBC said was in tribute to the yellow ribbons and flowers left at her mother's home, as well as worn by journalists in her newsroom."
model_output:
According to reports from NBC and various news outlets, Savannah Guthrie returned to the *Today* show on April 6, 2026, after a two-month absence following the disappearance and presumed abduction of her mother, Nancy Guthrie. 

For her return, Savannah wore a bright yellow dress as a tribute to the yellow ribbons and flowers that neighbors, friends, and supporters had placed around her mother's home in Tucson, Arizona. The color yellow became a symbol of hope and solidarity for her family during the ongoing search. 

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

(A) Approximately 40,000.
(B) Approximately 13,000.
(C) Approximately 56,000.
(D) Approximately 15,000.
(E) Approximately 67,000.
explanation: The article states: "The most recent count from winter 2025 reveals a continuing decline... estimated there were about 13,000- the lowest count since the 1970s."
model_output:
According to reports from NOAA Fisheries released in June 2025, the estimated population count for the Eastern North Pacific gray whale species in the winter of 2025 was approximately 13,000. This marked a continuing decline and the lowest abundance recorded since the 1970s, following an Unusual Mortality Event (UME) that severely impacted the population. 

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is April 6, 2026. The Artemis II crew will experience a period of radio silence as they pass behind the Moon. What will the astronauts be doing during this 40-minute blackout?
(A) Praying and sending good thoughts to the people of Earth.
(B) Testing the new European Space Agency Moonlight satellite network.
(C) Conducting lunar observation, taking images, and studying geology.
(D) Reading Michael Collins' 1974 memoir, Carrying the Fire.
(E) Pinpointing their position using the Goonhilly Earth Station antenna.
explanation: The article states: "They'll spend the blackout dedicated to lunar observation – taking images, studying the Moon's geology and simply gazing at its splendour."
model_output:
During the Artemis II mission's 40-minute communications blackout on April 6, 2026, the Orion spacecraft will pass behind the far side of the Moon, physically blocking all radio and laser signals to Earth. 

Even though they will be completely isolated from Mission Control, the astronauts will not be idle. They will spend this time dedicated to **lunar observation—taking images, studying the Moon's geology, and documenting the lunar surface**. The crew will use the spacecraft's windows and onboard devices to capture measurements and notes of the far side of the Moon, which has rarely been seen by the naked human eye. 

*(Note: While Pilot Victor Glover did mention praying and sending good thoughts, he suggested that the people of Earth use the 40 minutes of silence to send good thoughts and prayers up to the crew, rather than the other way around.)*

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Hallucination Herald] (https://hallucinationherald.com/artemis-ii-crew-enters-40-minutes-of-silence-as-they-pass-behind-the-moons-far-side)
- [Mashable] (https://mashable.com/article/artemis-2-moon-flyby-earthset-eclipse)
- [NASA] (https://www.nasa.gov/missions/artemis/artemis-ii/artemis-ii-flight-day-6-crew-ready-for-lunar-flyby/)

(A) They were unable to take off and were destroyed by US forces.
(B) They were shot down by Iranian surface-to-air missiles (SAMs).
(C) They were hit by small arms fire but landed safely in Kuwait.
(D) They were used to extract the pilot of a downed A-10 Warthog.
(E) They successfully transported the airman to a base in Kuwait.
explanation: The article states: "Two transport planes that were intended to fly out rescue crews were unable to take off from a remote base inside Iran, and were then destroyed to keep them out of enemy hands..."
model_output:
During the April 2026 rescue operation for the downed F-15E Strike Eagle crew in Iran, U.S. forces utilized multiple aircraft to extract the missing weapons systems officer and the commandos. According to reports, two MC-130J special operations transport planes intended to fly the rescue crews and the airman to safety malfunctioned and became stuck at a remote airstrip in Iran. To prevent the aircraft from falling into Iranian hands, U.S. commanders ordered the two disabled transport planes to be intentionally blown up and destroyed by U.S. forces. Replacement aircraft were then flown in to successfully extract the personnel. 

While Iranian state media claimed their forces shot down the transport planes and helicopters, U.S. officials confirmed the transport planes were deliberately destroyed after failing on the ground. 

Therefore, the correct option is (A).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

- Task & Purpose (https://taskandpurpose.com)
